articles.title: Vijay oath-taking confusion: Governor’s role, floor test rules and past Supreme Court judgment
articles.summary: The article examines the role of the Governor in appointing a Chief Minister when a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, citing the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983). It highlights the constitutional and procedural rules governing such situations, including the requirement for a floor test. The discussion is relevant to India's governance framework and may influence future political appointments and legislative procedures.
articles.raw_summary: As per the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983), which discusses the role of the governor, if a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, the leader of that party should automatically be asked to become the Chief Minister.

The court's order aims to teach students about the constitutional journey through the lives of key figures. This directive highlights the importance of recognizing Dr. Ambedkar's contributions beyond caste-based perceptions, ensuring his legacy is part of national education.
articles.raw_summary: The tragedy of our times lies in confining such a towering national figure within narrow caste-based perceptions. Dr Ambedkar s larger-than-life legacy is too often reduced to a sectional emblem, when in truth he belongs to the entire nation, to every classroom, every court, every institution of learning, and every citizen who cherishes dignity under the law. The central government will introduce The Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026 in Parliament. The last increase in the court's strength was from 30 to 33 in 2019.

articles.title: Bihar Cabinet approves $500 million loan from World Bank for strengthening urban centres
articles.summary: The Bihar Cabinet approved a $500 million loan from the World Bank to strengthen urban centers. The funds will support AI-based road maintenance, boost subsidies under the PM-eBus Sewa scheme, and finance Ganga erosion control works. Additionally, a hospital in Sitamarhi was renamed. These decisions aim to improve urban infrastructure and public services in Bihar.
articles.raw_summary: Bihar Cabinet okays $500 million World Bank loan for urban projects, boosts PM-eBus Sewa subsidy, funds AI based road upkeep, renames Sitamarhi hospital, clears Ganga erosion works.

articles.title: PF & ESI Disallowance Removed as Income Tax Act 2025 allows Deposit till ITR Due Date
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act 2025 now allows employers to deposit employee contributions to Provident Fund (PF) and Employees' State Insurance (ESI) until the due date for filing Income Tax Returns (ITR). Previously, even a one-day delay in depositing these contributions resulted in permanent tax disallowance. This change provides relief to businesses by removing the disallowance if payments are made before the ITR due date.
articles.raw_summary: Under the earlier law, even a one-day delay in depositing employee contributions resulted in permanent tax disallowance. The new regime provides relief where payments are completed before the due date for filing returns. The post PF   ESI Disallowance Removed as Income Tax Act 2025 allows Deposit till ITR Due Date appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Why Madras HC ordered granting full maternity leave to judicial employee for third pregnancy
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that a judicial employee should receive full maternity leave for her third pregnancy, criticizing a 12-week limit as discriminatory. The court stated that suffering during pregnancy is the same regardless of the number of pregnancies and that a welfare state cannot deny equal benefits. The ruling highlights the need for equitable maternity leave policies in government employment.
articles.raw_summary: The court criticised a 12-week maternity leave limit on third pregnancies, saying 'suffering would be the same' and a 'welfare state' cannot discriminate in approving maternity benefits.

This allows them to proceed with raising funds through public issues.

The new form is now available for submissions through the official portal.

articles.title: Section 16(2)(c) of CGST Act: A Constitutional Quagmire & Bona Fide Purchaser’s ‘Catch-22’
articles.summary: The article examines Section 16(2)(c) of India's Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act, which requires GST recipients to pay suppliers within 180 days to claim input tax credit (ITC). However, if suppliers default, compliant taxpayers still face ITC denial, creating a legal and practical dilemma. The analysis argues that this provision imposes an unfair burden on honest businesses while exposing them to unintended penalties.
articles.raw_summary: The analysis highlights how GST law forces recipients to pay suppliers within 180 days while still exposing them to ITC denial if suppliers default. It contends that the provision imposes an impossible burden on compliant taxpayers. The post Section 16(2)(c) of CGST Act: A Constitutional Quagmire   Bona Fide Purchaser s  Catch-22  appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The central government has introduced a new nationwide framework to regulate transfers of central land. The policy aims to reduce disputes and boost revenue by mandating separate valuations for buildings, clear guidelines for public versus commercial use, and requiring cabinet approval for transfers to private entities or states. This is expected to enhance transparency and unlock funds for public projects.

articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that entire bank deposits cannot automatically be treated as unexplained income in cases involving accommodation entry networks. articles.title: Section 271C Penalty Deleted as Proceedings Were Barred by Section 275(1)(c) Limitation
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Raipur ruled that penalty proceedings under Section 271C were invalid because they were initiated after the statutory limitation period under Section 275(1)(c) had expired. The tribunal held that delayed penalty orders cannot stand once the limitation period lapses. This decision impacts tax enforcement timelines and compliance expectations for taxpayers and authorities.

articles.raw_summary: DPIIT issues revised SOP for FDI approvals, mandates fully digital filing, sets 12 week decision timeline, tighter scrutiny for land border countries, and new FDI cells in ministries.

articles.title: Plea In Supreme Court Challenges Mizoram Inheritance Law For Excluding Mizo Women Marrying Non-Mizo Men
articles.summary: A writ petition has been filed in the Supreme Court challenging amendments to the Mizo Marriage and Inheritance of Property (Amendment) Act, 2026. The petition argues that the law discriminates against Mizo women who marry non-Mizo men and affects the rights of their children. The amendments to Sections 2, 3(m), 25, and 26(1) of the 2014 Act are being contested for alleged unconstitutional discrimination. The case highlights issues of gender equality and inheritance rights under tribal laws.

articles.title: Consumers Cannot Be Made To Bear Power Plant Depreciation Costs For Period During Which No Electricity Was Supplied: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that electricity consumers cannot be forced to pay depreciation costs for power plants that no longer supply them electricity, even if the plants have remaining technical life. The bench observed that consumers should not bear costs for services they no longer receive, citing a case where electricity supply ceased after March 2018. This decision provides relief to consumers and sets a legal precedent for similar disputes in the energy sector.
articles.raw_summary: In a significant relief to the electricity consumers, the Supreme Court on Thursday (May 7) has observed that electricity consumers cannot be burdened with paying depreciation costs for a power plant that no longer supplies electricity to them, even if the asset continues to have remaining technical life. “…admittedly, electricity has not been supplied to the consumers beyond March-2018. The consumers cannot be required to pay for a service which they no longer received…” , observed a bench of J

The tribunal emphasized that commercial wisdom, not judicial review, should guide such decisions, setting a precede
articles.raw_summary: NCLAT upholds NCLT decision, says no material irregularity in insolvency resolution process, creditors under ‘no obligation’ to approve plan with highest financial offer.

articles.summary: The article explains how India's new labour codes may impact gratuity benefits for employees. Currently, gratuity is a statutory payment given to employees after five years of continuous service with the same employer. The piece explores whether the new rules will maintain or increase gratuity amounts.
articles.raw_summary: At its core, gratuity continues to be a statutory payment made by an employer when the employee exits after meeting certain conditions. In most cases, employees need to complete five years of continuous service with the same employer to become eligible.

articles.title: Who Is Responsible For Filing Return of Income?
articles.summary: Taxpayers in India who wish to carry forward business or capital losses must file their income tax returns by the due date under Section 139(1) of the Income Tax Act. Failure to do so may result in losing the ability to carry forward these losses for future tax benefits. The requirement applies to all eligible taxpayers seeking to claim these deductions.

articles.title: ITAT Restricts Bogus Purchase Addition Due to Lack of Independent Enquiry by AO
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the full disallowance of purchases by the Assessing Officer (AO) was unjustified when sales and stock records were accepted. The ITAT reduced the disallowance to 12.5% of the disputed purchases due to the AO's lack of an independent enquiry. This decision impacts tax assessment practices and highlights the need for thorough verification in tax proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that full disallowance of purchases was not justified where corresponding sales and quantitative stock records were accepted. ITAT sustained only estimated disallowance at 12.5% of disputed purchases. The post ITAT Restricts Bogus Purchase Addition Due to Lack of Independent Enquiry by AO appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: States told to shift to new base year for GSDP by end of FY27
articles.summary: The statistics ministry has instructed all states to adopt 2022-23 as the new base year for calculating Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) by the end of fiscal year 2027. This change aims to align state-level economic data with the revised national GDP framework, improving accuracy. States are also permitted to develop sub-state Consumer Price Index (CPI) measures, though no central directive mandates this.
articles.raw_summary: The statistics ministry has directed states to adopt 2022-23 as the new base year for Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) computation by fiscal 2027. This move aligns state-level estimates with the revised national GDP framework, enhancing regional economic data accuracy. While states are also free to develop sub-state CPI, there's no central directive for this.

articles.title: No Addition, No Under-Reporting: ITAT Deletes Section 270A Penalty When Income Returned u/s 148 Was Fully Accepted
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that no penalty for under-reporting income can be imposed if the Assessing Officer accepts the income declared in response to a Section 148 notice without any additions. The Tribunal deleted the entire penalty under Section 270A in this case. This decision clarifies the legal interpretation of tax penalties and reinforces the principle that penalties require actual under-reporting, not just a reassessment.
articles.raw_summary: Pune ITAT held that penalty for under-reporting of income cannot survive where the Assessing Officer accepts the income declared in response to notice under Section 148 without any addition. The Tribunal deleted the entire penalty under Section 270A. The post No Addition, No Under-Reporting: ITAT Deletes Section 270A Penalty When Income Returned u/s 148 Was Fully Accepted appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.context_summary: India’s retail inflation likely rose to 3.8% in April from 3.4% in March as higher fuel and LPG costs linked to the US-Iran conflict started feeding into prices, according to a Reuters poll. Economists said food prices remained soft, helping keep inflation near the RBI’s 4% target, but persistently high crude oil prices and a possible weak monsoon could push inflation higher in coming months. Wholesale inflation is also expected to rise to 4.4% in April.

He notes this could ultimately push states to borrow more.

He noted that a large portion of state revenues is already committed towards salaries, pensions, interest payments and subsidies, leaving limited room for discretionary spending.

articles.title: UP cop alleges systemic corruption in Lucknow Police, appeals to CM Yogi for impartial inquiry
articles.summary: A Lucknow Police constable has publicly accused senior officers in the Lucknow Police Commissionerate of systemic corruption. In a video appeal to Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath, the constable alleged that the officers named in his complaints are overseeing the ongoing departmental inquiry, demanding instead an independent investigation. The constable questioned the credibility of the internal probe and sought external oversight to ensure fairness.

articles.title: India Cannot Sell Its Cotton to the World Until It Admits Why the World Stopped Buying
articles.summary: India's cotton sector has a productivity problem, a quality problem and a market-credibility problem. On Tuesday, the Union Cabinet approved a Rs 5,659.22 crore mission that proposes to address all three simultaneously, through seeds, ginning modernisation, traceability infrastructure and a national branding programme. What the mission does not address may be more revealing than what it does.

The bloc is also addressing cybersecurity threats from advanced AI models.

articles.title: Recycled Activewear Reveals the Structural Limits of Fashion’s Sustainability Claims
articles.summary: The article examines the sustainability claims of recycled polyester in activewear, noting that most material still comes from PET drink bottles rather than discarded textiles. This raises concerns about whether fashion brands are truly addressing waste or merely shifting it to other recycling systems. The issue has implications for global textile waste management and could influence future environmental and trade policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Recycled polyester has become a default sustainability claim across activewear, swimwear and outerwear, but its supply chain is less circular than its labelling suggests. Most of the material still comes from PET drink bottles, not discarded textiles, raising difficult questions about whether fashion is solving waste or diverting it from better-functioning recycling systems globally instead.

This ruling impacts how anti-profiteering cases under GST are handled.

articles.title: Bakery Products Sold Without Preparation Are Supply of Goods Under GST: Gujarat AAR
articles.summary: The Gujarat Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that bakery items like pizzas, pasta, salads, and shakes prepared or blended at outlets upon customer order are classified as restaurant services under GST, even when sold via takeaway counters. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of such products, distinguishing them from pre-packaged goods. The ruling applies to items prepared on-demand, impacting how bakery outlets are taxed for these services.

The ruling applies even where the food is sold through takeaway counters. The post Bakery Products Sold Without Preparation Are Supply of Goods Under GST: Gujarat AAR appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Sugarcane FRP hiked to Rs 365/quintal for 2026-27 season
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for sugarcane to ₹365 per quintal for the 2026-27 season. Farmers will receive an additional ₹3.56 per quintal for every 0.1% increase in sugar recovery above this level, with reductions for lower recovery. This decision impacts sugarcane farmers and the sugar industry, aligning pricing with productivity incentives.

articles.title: Services, retail lending drives banks’ credit growth to nearly 16% in FY26
articles.summary: India's banking sector saw credit growth of nearly 16% in FY26, driven by strong retail and services lending. Personal loans grew 16.2%, supported by vehicle financing and gold-backed loans, while housing credit remained stable. Personal loans accounted for about a third of total credit. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) may monitor these trends for potential policy adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: Retail lending remained strong, with personal loans rising 16.2%, up from 11.7% a year earlier, supported by demand for vehicle financing and gold-backed loans, while housing credit growth remained steady. Personal loans accounted for about a third of total credit.

articles.title: Thali costs rise in April on higher tomato, vegetable oil, LPG prices
articles.summary: The cost of home-cooked vegetarian and non-vegetarian thalis in India rose by 2% year-on-year in April, driven by higher prices of tomatoes, vegetable oil, and LPG. The article highlights inflationary pressures on essential food items, which may prompt government intervention.

The incident highlights risks to global energy security, which could influence India's oil imports and diplomatic strategies.
